title: Exclude investigator beads from corrective bead creation
description:
When an investigator bead is blocked, the scheduler incorrectly creates a corrective developer bead to fix it. Corrective beads make no sense for investigator beads — they are read-only analysis tasks, not implementation work, and a developer corrective cannot meaningfully unblock a failed investigation (which should simply be retried or requeued).

Fix `_can_plan_corrective` in `src/agent_takt/scheduler/followups.py` to return `False` when the bead's `agent_type` is `"investigator"`. Add the check at the top of the method alongside the existing corrective/recovery exclusions:

```python
def _can_plan_corrective(self, bead: Bead) -> bool:
    if self._is_corrective_bead(bead):
        return False
    if bead.agent_type == "investigator":
        return False
    ...
```

Also add a test in the scheduler followup tests verifying that a blocked investigator bead does not trigger corrective bead creation.
acceptance_criteria: A blocked investigator bead does not produce a corrective child bead
Existing corrective bead behaviour for other agent types is unchanged
